sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è risk-off a causa dell'aumento delle tensioni geopolitiche in Medio Oriente e dell'impatto sulla produzione di petrolio. Il VIX è sopra 20, il DXY è forte e il prezzo del petrolio è in spike. I mercati azionari sono chiusi per festività, ma i future indicano una possibile apertura in ribasso.
sentiment.reason_en: Market sentiment is risk-off due to rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and its impact on oil production. VIX is above 20, DXY is strong, and oil prices are spiking. Equity markets are closed for Easter Friday, but futures suggest a possible lower opening.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato è influenzato dalle tensioni in Medio Oriente, con il prezzo del petrolio che è salito del 7,78% a $109,03. Il DXY è salito dello 0,15% a 100,18, mentre il VIX è sceso del 2,73% a 23,87. Le crypto sono miste, con Bitcoin che è salito dello 0,14% a $66,981.
market_impact_summary.en: The market is impacted by Middle East tensions, with oil prices up 7.78% to $109.03. DXY rose 0.15% to 100.18, while VIX fell 2.73% to 23.87. Cryptos are mixed, with Bitcoin up 0.14% to $66,981.

audio_script_it:
Buongiorno e benvenuti all'aggiornamento finanziario di oggi, il vostro Morning Briefing quotidiano.

Mentre le borse mondiali osservano la consueta pausa festiva per la celebrazione di Venerdì Santo, i mercati asiatici hanno chiuso la scorsa notte con il Nikkei in calo del 2.38% a 52,463 punti e lo Shanghai Composite in flessione dello 0.74% a 3919.28 punti.

La chiusura dei mercati azionari e obbligazionari occidentali ieri ha visto l'S&P 500 guadagnare debolmente l'0.11% a 6582.69 punti. Il Dollar Index (DXY) ha registrato un aumento dello 0.15% a 100.18. Il VIX, indice di volatilità, è sceso del 2.73% a 23.87. Sul fronte dei tassi, il rendimento del decennale USA è sceso di 0.14 punti base al 4.31%.

La tensione geopolitica, in particolare il rischio di un escalation nel conflitto in Medio Oriente, continua a influenzare i mercati. L'aumento del prezzo del petrolio, con il Brent che sale del 7.78% a $109.03, riflette le preoccupazioni sulla potenziale interruzione delle forniture.

Sul fronte macroeconomico, gli investitori attendono con ansia i prossimi dati, in particolare il PIL del primo trimestre degli Usa, previsto per il 29 aprile. La BCE ha mantenuto i tassi di interesse invariati nella riunione di ieri.

Passiamo ora al comparto digitale, che opera 24 ore su 24. Bitcoin ha registrato un piccolo aumento dello 0.14% a $66,981. La capitalizzazione del mercato crypto continua a essere influenzata dal sentiment di risk-off, con l'indice Fear & Greed che si trova a 9, in territorio di Extreme Fear.

In attesa della ripresa degli scambi sui mercati tradizionali, gli investitori continueranno a monitorare l'evoluzione della situazione geopolitica e i prossimi dati economici. Restate sintonizzati per ulteriori aggiornamenti.

Spostiamo l'attenzione sul mercato delle criptovalute, dove abbiamo visto alcune mosse interessanti nelle ultime ore.

Bitcoin è sceso leggermente, portandosi a $66,986 con una perdita dello 0.13%. Gli ETF Bitcoin hanno registrato un deflusso di $173.7M, secondo i dati più recenti. Questo potrebbe indicare una certa debolezza nel mercato, ma è importante tenere d'occhio i flussi di denaro per capire meglio la situazione.

Tra le altcoin, Ethereum è scesa dello 0.54% a $2,054, mentre Solana e Binance Coin hanno mostrato una certa resilienza, con guadagni del 1.74% e 1.54% rispettivamente, portandosi a $80.52 e $588.03. Questi movimenti suggeriscono che gli investitori stanno diversificando i loro portafogli, cercando opportunità in altre criptovalute.

L'indice Fear & Greed è sceso a 9, livello di Estrema Paura. Questo potrebbe indicare che gli investitori sono diventati più cauti, possibilmente a causa dell'incertezza macroeconomica e delle notizie recenti dal mercato crypto. La correlazione con i dati macroeconomici, come il tasso di disoccupazione e l'inflazione, potrebbe influenzare ulteriormente il sentiment degli investitori.

In generale, il mercato delle criptovalute sembra essere in una fase di consolidamento, con gli investitori che stanno valutando attentamente le loro mosse. La prossima uscita dei dati macroeconomici, come il PIL degli Stati Uniti, potrebbe avere un impatto significativo sul mercato.

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.

Global sentiment is risk-off today as investors await further developments on the geopolitical front. Key data points include a decline in equities overnight and a rise in the Dollar Index. The VIX index is slightly lower at 23.87.

Traditional markets are closed today for the Good Friday holiday. At the last close, equities were mixed: the S&P 500 was up 0.11% to 6582.69, while the STOXX 600 was down 0.20% to 59.24. In the bond market, the US 10-year yield was down 0.14% to 4.31%, and the TLT Bond USA 20Y was up 0.61% to 86.79. The Dollar Index was up 0.15% to 100.18, and the Euro to US Dollar exchange rate was down 0.58% to 1.15. Commodities saw Brent crude up 7.78% to $109.03, while Gold was down 2.75% to $4651.50 per ounce.

Geopolitical tensions remain a focus with ongoing concerns about the situation in Iran. Fund managers are buying bonds on growth threat from Iran war. This has led to a flight to safety in the bond market and a rise in oil prices. The macroeconomic implications of this conflict are significant, and investors are advised to keep a close eye on developments.

While traditional markets observe their holiday break, the spotlight remains on digital assets, which are open 24/7. Let's take a look at the current state of the crypto market.

Moving on to the digital assets space...

The Bitcoin price has been hovering around $66,986, a slight decrease of 0.13% over the past 24 hours. From a technical standpoint, the recent BTC ETF daily net inflow of -$173.7M suggests a significant outflow, which could be a bearish signal. However, it's essential to consider this in the context of the broader market trends.

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and take a look at some of the major altcoins. Ethereum is trading at $2,054, down 0.54%, while Solana and Binance Coin are showing some resilience with gains of 1.74% and 1.54%, respectively. Solana's price increase might be attributed to its growing adoption and use cases. Binance Coin, on the other hand, has been steadily rising, potentially driven by the overall growth of the Binance ecosystem.

Now, let's discuss market sentiment. The Fear & Greed Index is currently at 9, indicating extreme fear. This could be a sign that investors are overly pessimistic, which sometimes leads to a contrarian buying opportunity. However, it's crucial to consider this in conjunction with other market indicators. Historically, extreme fear has been a buying signal, but it's essential to do thorough research and consider multiple factors before making any investment decisions.

The cryptocurrency market is highly correlated with the global economic landscape. With the recent macroeconomic data releases, investors are becoming increasingly cautious. The US economy's performance and the Federal Reserve's decisions will likely have a significant impact on the crypto market in the coming weeks. As we approach the next major economic announcements, it's essential to stay informed and adapt to the changing market conditions.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another market update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A quick look at what's on the radar for tomorrow and we'll wrap up here. Have a great day and we'll catch you soon. Goodbye.

articles.title: The Iran War and the Energy Lesson We Failed to Learn
articles.snippet: How many energy crises will it take before we stop betting our economies on a fragile oil market?The U.S. and Israeli war with Iran quickly morphed from a regional conflict into a global energy shock. Oil and gas markets have been volatile since the start of the war, with Brent crude oil prices rising 59 percent since Iran’s effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world’s most important energy chokepoints. In the first half of 2025, about one-fifth of the world’s oil and liquified natural gas passed through the strait. Now, five weeks into the conflict, attacks The post The Iran War and the Energy Lesson We Failed to Learn appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: The realities of economic security
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics presents a high-level analytical discussion of the realities of economic security, examining real-world trade-offs and vulnerabilities in today's geopolitically fractured world. Anjali Bhatt, host of PIIE Insider Live, moderates a candid discussion with PIIE experts: Warwick McKibbin, who presents global macroeconomic scenarios of the impact of the Middle East conflict; Cullen Hendrix, who discusses vulnerabilities of the global economy to the disrupted supply of commodities including food, fuel, and industrial inputs; and Jacob Funk…

articles.title: Climate change and central banking: macroeconomic challenges and evidence from Africa
articles.snippet: Climate change is an important source of macroeconomic risk with direct implications for monetary policy and financial stability. Physical risks, including more frequent and severe weather events, disrupt production and damage infrastructure, generating supply and demand side pressures that affect output and inflation.

articles.title: From Filament to Firepower: 3D Printing’s Impact on Warfare
articles.snippet: While much ink has been spilled over how 3D printing has enabled intense drone-on-drone warfare in Ukraine, the U.S. defense and intelligence communities have overlooked a stealthier development: Additive manufacturing is revolutionizing how guns are produced, fielded, and sustained in armed conflicts, especially by non-state actors. What once required a web of smuggling networks, foreign sponsors, and captured stockpiles can now be made with digital files and off-the-shelf parts. Even ammunition production, once considered an insurmountable barrier, is increasingly possible. This revolution in how arms and ammunition are manufactured has yielded resilient and decentralized supply chains that can endure both The post From Filament to Firepower: 3D Printing’s Impact on Warfare appeared firs

articles.title: When Fools Go to War
articles.snippet: One need only look past the moral and strategic differences between Iran and Ukraine to see that both are facing similar situations. Both have been attacked by larger powers whose institutional decline has produced regimes that failed to anticipate what they were setting into motion.

articles.title: Oil Rally Accelerates as Traders Price in Real Supply Disruption
articles.snippet: May WTI crude oil surged sharply in the holiday-shortened week ending April 3, settling at $111.54, up $11.90 or +11.94%. The move was driven by a combination of geopolitical escalation, supply disruption risks, and growing concerns about global oil infrastructure security. Traders were forced to rapidly reprice risk, with volatility spiking across the energy complex as headlines intensified into the close. War Risk and Supply Shock Drive Breakout The dominant catalyst behind this week’s rally was the rapid escalation in tensions between…

articles.title: First Western European Vessel Transits Hormuz Since War Began
articles.snippet: A container ship owned by French shipping giant CMA CGM, has just transited the Strait of Hormuz in what appears to be the first vessel linked to Western Europe to have successfully braved the critical chokepoint since the war in the Middle East began. The Malta-flagged CMA CGM Kribi, which openly broadcasts its French ownership, transited the Strait of Hormuz between Thursday afternoon and Friday morning, according to vessel-tracking data monitored by Bloomberg. Data on MarineTraffic showed that CMA CGM Kribi was in the Oman Gulf as of 12 p.m.…

articles.title: Trump Loses Grip as Oil Surge Signals Deeper Crisis
articles.snippet: For the first time in weeks, Donald Trump failed to cool oil markets, as escalating tensions with Iran crushed ceasefire hopes and reignited bullish momentum. Friday, April 03, 2026 For the first time in many weeks, US President Trump couldn’t contain a late-week price surge as escalating tensions between Washington and Tehran dulled market bears’ expectations of a ceasefire anytime soon. WTI soared this week and is on par with ICE Brent (even if they trade different months), physical benchmarks above $140 per barrel bring back memories…

articles.title: UAE's Biggest Gas Plant Forced Offline for Second Time Since War Began
articles.snippet: Operations at the Habshan gas facilities, the biggest gas processing site in the United Arab Emirates, were suspended early on Friday following a fire that erupted after an attack, Abu Dhabi Media Office said. The Habshan onshore facilities are part of one of the world's largest gas processing plants, which is operated by Abu Dhabi's national oil company ADNOC. The five plants of the vast Habshan Complex have 14 processing trains and 6.1 bscfd capacity, according to the company. This is the second time operations at Habshan have been suspended…

articles.title: Canada’s Synthetic Crude Soars 200% as War Chokes Diesel Supply
articles.snippet: The price of Canada’s synthetic crude, which has rich diesel yield for refiners, has surged by nearly 200% since March 27 as the Middle East war cripples global diesel supply and sends diesel futures, cracks, and retail prices soaring. The synthetic crude made from processing of bitumen from Alberta’s oil sands is very low sulfur and its chemical content makes it highly suitable for processing into jet fuel and diesel—the most stressed barrels amid the worst disruption to supply in the history of the oil market. Canada’s…

articles.title: Circle to launch cirBTC wrapped Bitcoin, challenging BitGo and Coinbase
articles.snippet: Circle, known for issuing stablecoins including USDC and EURC, is expanding into the Bitcoin space, targeting institutional users.

articles.title: Rocky US economy, private credit stress, war impact Bitcoin’s odds for $75K rally
articles.snippet: Are Bitcoin’s odds for a rally to $75,000 diminished by a weakening US economy, the war in Iran and multiple institutional BTC holders selling in the open market?
articles.title_it: Rocky US economy, private credit stress, war impact Bitcoin’s odds for $75K rally

articles.title: Bitcoin miner Soluna acquires $53M wind farm to power AI facility
articles.snippet: Soluna expanded into artificial intelligence in 2024 to shore up declining revenues from its crypto mining business, mirroring an industry-wide shift toward AI workloads.
articles.title_it: Bitcoin miner Soluna acquires $53M wind farm to power AI facility
articles.title_en: Bitcoin miner Soluna acquires $53M wind farm to power AI facility
